name: Marquise Chocolate Mousse Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt the milk chocolate over a double boiler, add the praline and chopped almonds. Spread on wax paper and freeze.
2. Whip egg whites and sugar until stiff peaks form. Gently incorporate egg yolks, then mix in flour and cocoa powder.
3. Spread or pipe batter onto sheets larger than the cake ring.
4. Bake at 375-390°F (190°C) until set and firm to touch.
5. Chop bitter chocolate and chocolate for mousse. Whip cream until stiff peaks form, add truffles, butter, and gently fold in.
6. Boil sugar and water with rum; let cool slightly.
7. Cut rocher chocolate to fit the cake ring.
8. Place bottom layer on a flat surface, cover with cut chocolate, press down, and brush syrup.
9. Add mousse layers, top with remaining syrup-coated chocolate, and place in fridge for 1 hour.
10. Remove from ring.
11. Boil cream/glucose mixture until thick, add chopped chocolate, butter; mix and pour over chilled cake.
12. Spread glaze with spatula and decorate as desired.
(Note: Posted on rec.food.recipes by ah787@freenet.carleton.ca (Bill Stuart))
